channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This 10-second tweak will bring new life to any browser
item.description: When it comes to most home screens—on your phone, your computer, you name it—you’ve got endless-seeming options for customizing and taking control. The one canvas you can’t easily paint, curiously enough, is your browser’s New Tab page.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it’s the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Morga, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot could be the future of affordable in-home care
item.description: A humanoid robot made by a Chinese company proposes to redefine personalized elderly care and assistance for people with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massachusetts judge rules Meta must face state's social media lawsuit
item.description: A Massachusetts Superior Court judge has ruled Meta must face a state lawsuit, alleging the company knowingly designed and deployed features harmful to young online users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two Virginia congressional candidates debated an AI chatbot. It was shockingly rational
item.description: On Thursday night an AI chatbot participated in a political debate with candidates for a congressional seat, and, despite a few technical glitches, performed well. There was no robot voice, no off-topic tangents, no personal attacks, no odd “they’re eating the dogs” hallucinations.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: 25 year-old Alabama man arrested for January hack of SEC’s X account
item.description: An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Samsung’s $17 billion Texas chip factory faces another setback. Here’s why
item.description: Samsung Electronics has postponed taking deliveries of ASML chipmaking equipment for its upcoming factory in Texas as it has yet to win any major customers for the project, three people familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Right-wing influencers hyped anti-Ukraine videos made by a TV producer also funded by Russian media
item.description: It's a trend that concerns current and former U.S. intelligence officials and disinformation experts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: States grasping the benefits of, need for cyber harmonization
item.description: Public and private sector experts say programs like StateRAMP make their data and networks safer as cyber threats become more serious and resources sparse. The post States grasping the benefits of, need for cyber harmonization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: CIOs under pressure to deliver AI outcomes faster
item.description: CIOs are under increasing pressure to generate business value from generative AI, but Salesforce CIO Juan Perez is among those IT leaders who believe such impatience from the C-suite could doom many projects.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: El desarrollo de ‘software’ nativo de IA podría estar más cerca de lo que parece
item.description: Los desarrolladores no impresionados por los primeros resultados de la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa para la codificación toman nota: el desarrollo de software se dirige hacia una nueva era, en la que la mayor parte del código será escrito por agentes de IA y revisado por desarrolladores e
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Choosing the best AI models for your business
item.description: From digital assistants to code generation, many small to medium businesses (SMBs) harbor lofty ambitions for generative AI (GenAI). The next step, however, is just as significant: whether to build their AI initiatives from scratch or simply secure a quick win with an existing AI tool.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta partners with horror movie maker Blumhouse Productions to test Movie Gen
item.description: Facebook owner Meta said on Thursday it had teamed up with Blumhouse Productions, the Hollywood company behind popular horror films such as The Purge and Get Out, to put its new generative AI video model Movie Gen to the test.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Musk kicks off campaign swing for Trump in Pennsylvania
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk kicked off a campaign swing across Pennsylvania Thursday in support of former President Trump, underlining the importance of the battleground state in the rapidly nearing election.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: One Direction was the internet's first boy band, and Liam Payne its grounding force
item.description: One Direction member Liam Payne was found dead this week after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, Argentina, at just 31.
item.dc:creator: Maria Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Sextortion occurs when a victim is coerced to provide sexually explicit photos or videos of themselves and threatened with them being shared publicly.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: How to use Task.WhenEach in .NET 9
item.description: The upcoming release of .NET 9 introduces the Task.WhenEach method, which enables developers to handle asynchronous tasks more elegantly. The Task.WhenEach method allows us to iterate through tasks as they complete, without having to call Task.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Big Tech turns to nuclear energy to power AI boom
item.description: Major technology companies are increasingly turning to nuclear energy as they attempt to keep up with the vast energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI) without derailing their highly publicized environmental goals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Trump thinks Google split would weaken US against China
item.description: The potential split up of Google that’s been proposed by the US Department of Justice (DOJ) could weaken the company, and thus the position of the US in its tech war with China, said former President Donald Trump, who suggested he may not break up the company if he wins the presidency again in Novem
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 15 new Android 15 features to find on your Google Pixel phone
item.description: Oh, hello Android 15. How’s that for an all-around excellent October surprise? Yes, indeedly: Google’s latest and greatest Android version is officially on its way to Pixel devices near and far this week, just over a month after Google dropped the source code in an unusually detached move.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: End user empowerment: Shifting the cybersecurity responsibility
item.description: After 21 years of Cybersecurity Awareness Months, maybe it’s time to reconsider the burden we place on end users. The post End user empowerment: Shifting the cybersecurity responsibility first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI-powered scam targets Gmail users
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cisco pumps up data center networking with AI, large workloads in mind
item.description: Cisco is boosting network density support for its data center switch and router portfolio as it works to deliver the network infrastructure its customers need for cloud architecture, AI workloads and high-performance computing.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI-powered scam targets 2.5 billion Gmail users in sophisticated phishing attacks
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about a new AI-powered scam that targets Gmail users and is harder to detect unless you're very cautious.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Get started with the free-threaded build of Python 3.13
item.description: The single biggest new feature in Python 3.13 is something Python users have anticipated for ages: a version of Python that allows full concurrency between Python threads by removing the Global Interpreter Lock.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk loaded $75 million into his pro-Trump super PAC in 3 months
item.description: Elon Musk has personally contributed at least $75 million to his pro-Trump America PAC since July as he pushes for the former president to return to the White House, according to campaign finance records filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Intel, AMD unite in new x86 alliance to tackle AI, other challenges
item.description: Semiconductor rivals Intel and AMD announced the formation of an x86-processor advisory group that will try to address ever-increasing AI workloads, custom chiplets, and advances in 3D packaging and system architectures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World
